title: view-status command: start the viewer server if needed and open the browser to the status page
description: Ship a `to-execution view-status [target]` CLI verb. It checks the viewer-server Runtime Record (.excn/runtime pid record): if the server is running, reuse its port; if absent/stale, start the stamped viewer-server daemon the same way the SessionStart hook does. Then open the user's default browser to the status page URL (macOS `open`, with linux/win fallbacks). Prints the URL either way. Fail-closed CLI semantics (non-zero on a target with no stamped .excn), consistent with existing verbs and usage text; doctor's viewer_server card stays the health authority.
acceptance_criteria: `to-execution view-status` on a stamped Instance with no running server starts the daemon, prints the URL, and opens the browser to it.
With a server already running, it reuses the existing port (no second daemon) and opens the browser.
A stale pid record is treated as not-running (matches doctor's logic).
Non-stamped target exits non-zero with a clear message; usage/help text updated.
Works from the npm-installed package (node_modules/.bin/to-execution view-status).
Passes code-standards and package-qa gates; ships in the next npm version.
fix: view-status verb reuses-or-starts the viewer-server daemon from its pid Runtime Record (doctor-matching staleness) and opens the default browser to the status URL. Commit 487cdcf; gates PASS.
